Title: Chief PACE Setter
Bio: Mindy Price is the founder of Direct Effect Solutions and a leadership strategist with over 20 years of experience in organizational development and training. She helps public sector teams strengthen collaboration, problem-solving, and decision-making through practical tools and engaging facilitation. Known for her people-centered approach, she has worked with municipalities nationwide to elevate leadership at every level.

Title: People Development & Performance Manager
Bio: Terri Crowell serves as the People Development and Performance Manager for the City of Altamonte Springs, where she leads strategic initiatives focused on employee growth, leadership development, and organizational culture. Passionate about creating inclusive, high-performing workplaces, Terri helps shape programs such as New Supervisor Orientation (NSO), Supervisor Circuit, Supervisor Summit, and the City’s performance management platform, Perform. With extensive experience leading HR transformation across diverse organizations, she specializes in modernizing talent strategies, strengthening leadership capabilities, and aligning people-focused solutions with business goals. Terri is dedicated to building environments where employees feel valued, empowered, and equipped to succeed, while helping the City prepare for the future of work through innovation, adaptability, and people-first leadership.

Title: Director of Human Resources
Bio: Kendall Gow is the Director of Human Resources for the City of Altamonte Springs, officially sworn into the role in December 2025. With nearly a decade of HR experience, she leads initiatives that combine technology with people-focused strategy to improve efficiency and engagement. She has developed AI governance policies, delivered citywide training, and created Herman Resources—an AI-powered HR assistant. Kendall was also part of SHRM’s inaugural AI + HI credential program and is among the first in the country to earn certification in this emerging field.

Title: Speaker, Meteorologist, Author, Host
Bio: Amy Sweezey is a meteorologist, author, speaker, and Founder & CEO of Social Weather LLC. After spending her entire career in full-time broadcast news, Amy stepped away in 2020 to pursue her “What’s Next,” proving that reinvention has no expiration date. Today, she focuses on public speaking and providing media services for companies worldwide, with clients including Carnival Cruise Line, Growing Bolder, Convention News Television, and local affiliates of ABC, CBS, NBC, FOX, and Spectrum News across the country. Amy holds the prestigious Certified Broadcast Meteorologist seal from the American Meteorological Society and the National Weather Association Broadcast Seal of Approval. She is the recipient of multiple “Best Weathercast” and “Best Weather Reporting” awards from the Florida Associated Press and was named the 2018 National Weather Association “Broadcaster of the Year.” She is also the author of two children’s books about weather and two educational weather workbooks for elementary students. In 2024, Amy shared her story, Reinvention at Any Age, at TEDxEustis, and in 2025 she launched a podcast by the same name to inspire others on their own path to reinvention.

Title: Director of Growth Management
Bio: Dean S. Fathelbab, AICP, serves as Director of Growth Management for the City of Altamonte Springs, where he leads strategic planning and development initiatives that support smart, sustainable growth. Previously serving as Division Director of Development Services, Dean played a key role in shaping the City’s growth through development review operations, innovative planning strategies, and strong cross-department collaboration. In his current role, he champions data-driven city growth, connected infrastructure, and resilient design to help position Altamonte Springs for long-term success. Dean holds a Master of Science in Urban and Regional Planning and a Bachelor of Arts in Political Science from the University of Central Florida and is an active member of the American Institute of Certified Planners. Known for his collaborative leadership style and passion for progress, Dean continues to help drive Altamonte Springs’ vision for the future.

Title: City Manager
Bio: Frank has led the City of Altamonte Springs to national and international recognition for municipal innovation while maintaining one of Florida’s lowest tax rates, low utility costs, and more than 13 years of debt-free operations. Under his leadership, Altamonte Springs has been recognized by Money Magazine as one of the Top 50 U.S. Cities to Live and ranked by WalletHub as the 9th best city in the nation for small business. Frank has pioneered transformative public-private partnerships, including the nation’s first permanent city-led autonomous vehicle deployment, one of the first community-wide mobility programs with Uber, and the creation of pureALTA, an award-winning system that produces drinking water from reuse water. He also founded the Altamonte Springs Science Incubator (AS2I), Florida’s first city-led STEM program, impacting more than 35,000 students. His leadership continues through the Altamonte Global Innovation Lab (AGīL), advancing AI, mobility, sustainability, and smart city solutions. Recognized as one of Central Florida’s most influential leaders, Frank has been named one of the Top 10 Public Sector Transportation Innovators in the U.S. and remains a leading voice in innovation, technology, and public sector transformation.

Title: Risk Manager
Bio: Traci is an experienced Risk Manager with over 20 years in claims and risk management. For the past seven years, she has proudly served the City of Altamonte Springs, leading risk management strategies that help protect city staff and residents. Before joining the City, Traci spent 15 years in the private sector with a Fortune 100 insurance company, where she specialized in complex workers’ compensation and general liability claims before advancing to Claims Manager, overseeing major cases, litigation, and a high-performing regional team. She currently serves on the PRIMA Education Committee, received the 2024 BLOOM Excellence Award for championing workplace safety, risk, and human resource excellence, and has been a featured presenter on innovative risk management strategies. Outside of work, Traci enjoys traveling with her husband and has a strong passion for yoga and Pilates, which bring balance and focus to both her personal and professional life.

Title: Director of Leisure Services
Bio: Ryan Thompson serves as the Director of Leisure Services for the City of Altamonte Springs, bringing almost 30 years of experience in leisure and maintenance operations. He provides strategic oversight across multiple city services and holds a B.S. in Business & Information Management from Seminole State College. Ryan is dedicated to building strong organizational culture through mentorship, employee engagement, and talent development. His leadership focuses on collaboration, cross-departmental support, and delivering exceptional service to the community.

Title: Labor/Employment Partner
Bio: Alex is an attorney in the Orlando and Boston offices of Fisher Phillips and is an integral member of the firm’s Labor Relations Practice Group. Alex advises and represents employers across the country in traditional labor matters related to union organization, collective bargaining, grievance arbitrations, contract interpretation, work stoppages and unfair labor practices. He regularly appears before the National Labor Relations Board on behalf of employers, representing their interests in union election matters and defending them against claims of unfair labor practices.

Title: Deputy Police Chief
Bio: Evelyn Steenekamp serves as Deputy Chief of the Altamonte Springs Police Department, where she has dedicated more than 15 years to supporting and safeguarding the community. She holds a Bachelor of Science in Criminal Justice and Law Enforcement Administration from Columbia Southern University and brings extensive experience in police administration, community policing, and investigations. Committed to public safety and strong community relationships, Evelyn is passionate about fostering trust, integrity, and excellence both within the department and throughout the City of Altamonte Springs.

Title: Recreation Division Director
Bio: Neida Hernandez leads the City’s Recreation Division with over a decade of experience, focusing on innovative programs, community connection, and making recreation accessible and impactful for all.

Title: Watch Commander
Bio: Lieutenant Todd Smith is a distinguished member of the Police Department, proudly serving in his current leadership role since 2020. Since beginning his career in August 2005, he has dedicated two decades to public service, excelling in a wide range of roles across the department. Throughout his career, Lieutenant Smith has demonstrated an unwavering commitment to excellence, growth, and community impact. A passionate and dynamic public speaker, he brings energy and purpose to every engagement, striving to leave a lasting and meaningful impression on every audience he addresses.

Title: People Operations Coordinator
Bio: Robert McDaniel serves as the People Operations Coordinator for the City of Altamonte Springs, where he supports a wide range of HR operations and employee development initiatives. Since joining the City in December 2024, he has worked closely with the People Development and Performance team to help coordinate programs such as New Supervisor Orientation (NSO), Supervisor Circuit, Supervisor Summit, and other learning and development efforts. With a focus on process improvement, employee support, and organizational efficiency, Robert helps ensure HR operations run smoothly while contributing to a positive and connected workplace culture.
